title: alnus × peculiaris
intro: Alnus × peculiaris, also known as the hybrid green alder, is a cross between the green alder (Alnus viridis) and the black alder (Alnus glutinosa), resulting in a unique plant species with distinct characteristics. The bark and leaves of Alnus × peculiaris contain medicinal properties that have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Alnus × peculiaris include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Alnus × peculiaris is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
